volume.h1:
BOMBAY KARNATAK INSCRIPTIONS
VOL. I, PART II. [= South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. 11, Part 2]

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(B.K. No. 186 of 1926-27.)
NAREGAL, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB INSIDE THE TEMPLE OF KALAMĒŚVARA.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and places on record the gift of land made by Muṇḍeyara Śrī[va]nta-Gāvuṇḍa on the
specified date to Nīlakaṇṭha Paṇḍitadēva, for the benefit of the temple of
Mūlasthānadēva, built by him in the centre of the southern quarter of Narayaṁgal.
At the time of recording the grant the Sinda Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Permāḍidēvarasa was
ruling over Kisukāḍu Seventy, Keḷavāḍi Three-Hundred, Bāgaḍage Seventy and
Narayaṁgal Twelve, from his capital at Erambarage. It is known from a record at
Kōḍikop, a hamlet of Narēgal (B.K. No. 194 of 1926-27), that the chief was adminis-
tering the same tract in A.D. 1144 under the Western Chālukya king Jagadēkamalla
II (J.B.B.R.A.S., Vol. XI, pp. 253 ff.).

The charter is dated Śaka 872, Śaumya, Pushya, śu. puṇṇami, Monday, lunar
eclipse and Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. The details are very irregular. In Saumya'
according to the Southern Cycle, Pushya full-moon fell on Monday but the tithi ended
at ·06. There was no lunar eclipse or Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti on this day. But in the
previous year, i.e., Kīlaka, which was Saumya, according to the Northern Cycle
Pushya-puṇṇami commenced at ·11 on Monday; but there was no eclipse on this day
and the Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti occurred only five days later. But on the following,
i.e., Māgha-pūrṇimā, there was a lunar eclipse. Taking Saumya by the Northern
Cycle, the week-day and tithi would yield the English date Monday, December
18, A.D. 948.
(Published in J.B.B.R.A.S., Vol. XI, pp. 224 ff.).

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(B.K. No. 214 of 1926-27.)
YERIBYĀTĒRI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALIṄGA.
inscription.p:
This much damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla-
Permāḍidēva (Vikramāditya VI). It refers to a subordinate who belonged to the
[Pāpakalla-kula] and records a gift to the god of Bēlēṟa Jōgēśvarada Mūlasthāna
and to the god Bōḷēśvara. It mentions Chāvuṇḍa-Gāvuṇḍa, Jōma-Gāvuṇḍa,
Jōga-Gāvuṇḍa and Jakkeyabe.

The record is dated Śaka 968, Pārthiva, Paushya, śu. [2], Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṅkramaṇa. In Pārthiva, the saṅkramaṇa fell not on Sunday but on Tuesday
(= December 24, A.D. 1045). But in the previous year, i.e., Tāraṇa, it fell on Sunday,
Pausha, śu. 1 = Sunday, December 23, 1044 A.D. The latter appears to be the
more probable date of the record.

Since the date falls during the first year of the reign of Traiḷōkyamalla Sōmēś-
vara I, the father of Vikramāditya VI, it is not possible to account for the imperial
titles applied to the latter as early as A.D. 1044.

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1926.)
AMMINBHĀVI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE CEILING OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
OF THE JAIN BASTI.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription purports to belong to the reign of Tribhuvana-
malladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and refers to his subordinate Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Jayakēśidēva, who was then ruling over Koṅkaṇa Nine-Hundred, Palasige Twelve-
Thousand and Kundūr Five-Hundred from his capital at Gōvage (Goa). It seems
to record the foundation of a Śiva temple at Ammayanabhāvi, an agrahāra in
Kundūr-nāḍu and registers a gift of rice made for offerings in the temple by Māchi-
ya[ṇṇa] and others.

The record is dated Śaka 993, Virōdhakṛit, • • • •, paṁchamī, Sunday, vyatīpāta.
The month is lost. During the year, pañchamī combined with Sunday on the
following occasions:—
(1) Jyēshṭha, ba. 5 = May 22, A.D. 1071.
(2) Āshāḍha, śu. 5 = June 5, A.D. 1071.
(3) Nija-Āśvina, ba. 5 = October 16, A.D. 1071.
(4) Kārttika, śu. 5 = October 30, A.D. 1071.
(5) Phālguṇa, śu. 5 = February 26, A.D. 1071.
and (6) Phālguṇa, ba. 5 = March 11, A.D. 1072.

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(B.K. No. 27 of 1927-28.)
UṆACHGĒRI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE STANDING NEAR THE PUBLIC WELL.
inscription.p:
The inscription purports to belong to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI) and records the grant of a house to the god Kallidēva of Kūṇṭageyabīḍu,
by four Gāvuṇḍas and of land and money by (the body of) the Thousand, to the gods
Kallidēvasvāmi, Mādēśvaradēva and Kāmēśvaradēva.

The record is dated Śaka 994, Paridhāvin, Pushya, śu. 13, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṅkrānti. The probable equivalent of the date would be A.D. 1072, December 23,
Sunday. The tithi was not śu. 13, but śu. 11. It has to be remarked that the date of
this record is almost identical with that of B.K. No. 33 of 1927-28 above, belonging
to the reign of the king's predecessor Bhuvanaikamalladēva. (See foot note 2 on
p. 110 above.)

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(B.K. No. 46 of 1928-29.)
HUNGUND, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE CEILING OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALIṄGA.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and
is much damaged. It seems to register the gift of some land to a temple. It mentions
Bāhubali, the disciple of [Indra]ṇandi of the Mūla-saṅgha, Dēśīya-gaṇa, Pustaka-
gachchha and Kuṇḍakund-ānvaya, who built the temple.

The record is dated Śaka 9 • • • • Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. The details are
lost.

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(B.K. No. 237 of 1928-29.)
MORAB, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE KALAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription which belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI) describes his vassal, Traiḷōkyamalla Vīra-Noḷaṁba-Pallava-Permāḍi Jaya-
siṅghadēva ‘of the Pallava family’, who bears the epithets yuvarāja, rājaparamē-
śvara, and Chāḷukya-chūḍāmaṇi and refers to him as the king's brother. He held
the status of yuvarāja and was governing over [Kōgaḷe] One-Thousand, Kandūr One-
Thousand, Puligeṟe Three Hundred and Beḷuvala Three-Hundred. On the occasion
of the Mahādānas connected with the Tulāpurusha and the Hiraṇyagarbha ceremonies
performed by him, at the request of his subordinates Mahāpradhāna, Hērilāḷa-
Kannaḍa-sandhivigrahi, Daṇḍanāyaka Saṅkharayya-Nāyaka and Mahāpradhāna,
Sēnādhipati, Manevergaḍe, Daṇḍanāyaka Madhuvapayya-Nāyaka, the prince re-
named the big tank at Moraba in Beḷuvala-nāḍu as Noḷaṁba-samudra and granted
land for its maintenance, to the twelve Gāvuṇḍas of the village, as a Paramēśvara-
datti.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 3, Piṅgaḷa, Phāḷguṇa, puṇṇame,
Sunday, lunar eclipse. Piṅgaḷa was actually Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 2. In this
year, there was no lunar eclipse in Phālguṇa; but there was one in Śrāvaṇa, on śu. 15,
Sunday = A.D. 1077, August 6. Phālguṇa is evidently a mistake for Śrāvaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(B.K. No. 97 of 1929-30.)
MUTTIGI, BĀGEVĀḌI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE PROPER LEFT WALL OF THE SAṄGAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and records the construction of the temple of Rāmēśvara at Murttage and grant of
property, for its endowment, into the hands of the trustee Yōgēśvara-Paṇḍitadēva,
by the king's general Gōvindarāja and his younger sister Ponnakabbe. It also records
the gift, on a later occasion, of land, for the same temple, to Achaḷēśvara-Paṇḍitadēva
by Vishṇudēva alias Viṭṭa, the son of Gōvindarāja.

The record bears two dates. The first is Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 4, Siddhārtha,
Pushya, amāvāsye, Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, solar eclipse. The saṅkrānti
actually commenced on Tuesday, December 24, at ·79 and must have been observed
on Wednesday, on which day also amāvāsyā commenced at ·07. Considering the
coincidence of the amāvāsyā and the saṅkrānti, the intended date might be Wednes-
day, 25th December, A.D. 1079. But the solar eclipse occurred on the next day i.e.,
Thursday, December 26.

The second date, Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 35, Vikṛita, Kārttika, puṇṇami,
Vaḍḍavāra, lunar eclipse, regularly corresponds to Saturday, October 29, A.D. 1110.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XV, p. 26.)

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(B.K. No. 76 of 1927-28.)
DŌṆI, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE SIDDHALIṄGADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription which belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI) registers a gift of garden made by the Sixty Mahājanas and Seṭṭis of the
great agrahāra Drōṇapura, headed by the Ūroḍeya, to the temple of Siddhēśvaradēva,
while the chief queen Lakshmīdēvī was governing the town. The king is given the
biruda Rāyanārāyaṇa.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 5, Raudri, Pushya, śu. pāḍiva, Monday,
solar eclipse, vyatīpāta. The details correspond to A.D. 1080, December 14, Monday,
on which day pāḍiva commenced at ·11.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(B.K. No. 128 of 1926-27.)
KURTAKŌṬI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR A WELL CALLED UPPUNĪRINA-BHĀVI.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya.
VI) and registers a gift of income accruing from fines on offences such as
defamation, assault, etc., made, for the benefit of the Kaḍalegeṟe tank, to the Ūroḍeya
and the Two-Hundred Mahājanas of the agrahāra Kuṟitakūṁṭe by Mahā-
pradhāna, Manevergaḍe, Daṇḍanāyaka Vāmadēvayya, an officer under Mahā-
maṇḍalēśvara Trailōkyamalla Vīra-Noḻaṁba-Pallava Permāḍi Jayasiṅgadēva, ‘of the
Pallava family,’ who, as Yuvarāja, was ruling over Purigeṟe Three-Hundred,
Beḷuvala Three-Hundred, Banavāsi Twelve-Thousand and Kundūr One-Thousand.
The Ūroḍeya and the Two-Hundred Mahājanas also made a gift of land for the tank
and for the temple of Aṇuvēśvaradēva. The inscription was composed by Nāga-
dēva of the vipra-grāma Poṁboḷal, written by Kuḷakaraṇi Mābalārya and engraved
by Ayyaṇa, son of Kaṇṭada Bamma of the t own of Purikara.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 7, Dundubhi, Pushya, śu. 3, Sunday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, vyatīpāta, regularly corresponding to A.D. 1082, December
25, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1926.)
TĒGŪR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE VILLAGE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and refers to his subordinate Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gūvaladēva, who possessed the
‘five great sounds’, who was the lord of Banavāsipura and who belonged to the
family of Trilōchana-Kadaṁba. While the latter was governing over Koṅkaṇa Nine-
Hundred and Halasige Twelve-Thousand, Jayadēva-Gāvuṇḍa granted land for wor-
ship, offerings and repairs in the temple of Kalidēvasvāmin.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 7 • • • •, Pushya, śu. 5, Sunday,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. In Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 7 (Dundubhi), the Uttarāyaṇa-
saṅkrānti fell on Pushya śu. 3 (= Sunday, December 25, A.D. 1082). Śu. 5 is
probably a mistake for śu. 3.

mūkagāvuṇḍaṁ māḍisida kalidēvasvāmiya dēgulada jīrṇnōddhāra-

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1927-28.)
SAVAḌI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE LEANING AGAINST THE FRONT WALL OF THE
VILLAGE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
This slightly damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI). It describes at length the heroic qualities of the chief Āchugi and
his son Bhūlōkamalla Permāḍi, a Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara, who possessed the ‘five great
sounds’, who was worshipper of the feet of Bhūlōkamalladēva and was the ruler of
Kisukāḍu Seventy, Bāgaḍage Seventy and Nareyaṁgal Twelve. It registers the grant
of land made by Gaṅgara Bāchimayya to Chandrabhūshaṇa-Paṇḍita, for the benefit
of the temple of Mūlasthānadēva at Sayiḍi (called also Sayyaḍi) and for feeding
ascetics. The charity was to be administered by the Four-Hundred Mahājanas of
the place.

The inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 7, Dundubhi, Phālguṇa, ba.
pañchamī, Monday. Ba. pañchamī in the given month was not Monday but Saturday,
11th March, A.D. 1083.
1 The letters dandu are engraved above the line.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(B.K. No. 239 of 1926-27.)
SŪḌI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE TEMPLE OF BASAVAṆṆA.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI) and mentions his feudatory, Daṇḍanātha Śrīvallabha who was then
administering a district (probably Kisukāḍu Seventy). It records that this chief
granted the town of Mudiyanūr to a trustee (name lost) who was a votary of
Vigrahēśvara. The charity was placed in charge of Six Gāvuṇḍas and the
Eight Śeṭṭis.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 9, Raktākshi, Āśvayuja, puṇṇami,
Friday, lunar eclipse. The week day is a mistake for Monday, on which day occurred
the full-moon and the lunar eclipse. The corresponding English date would be A.D.
1084, September 16, Monday.
(Lines 4 to 7 published and the rest summarised in Ep. Ind., Vol. XV, pp. 104 f.).

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(B.K. No. 235 of 1926-27.)
SŪḌI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE ON THE LEFT SIDE OF THE VILLAGE GATEWAY.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalla-Vallabha (Vikramāditya
VI) and records the grant of the village Poṁgari in Kisukāḍu Seventy, by his queen
Lakshmādēvī, to Sōmēśvara-Paṇḍitadēva of the temple of Achalēśvara at rājadhānī
Sūṇḍi, for the benefit of the temple, for feeding ascetics and for imparting education.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 9, Raktākshi, Pushya, ba. navamī,
Tuesday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, which regularly corresponds to A.D. 1084, December
24, Tuesday.

(Lines 2 to 29 published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XV, pp. 100 ff., where an abstract of the
rest of the record is given. Since the record is much damaged no further
improvement is possible.)

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(B.K. No. 73 of 1929-30.)
TUṀBIGI, MUDDEBIHĀḶ TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE BUILT INTO A BASTION IN THE VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This inscription which is badly damaged belongs to the reign of Tribhuvana-
malladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and seems to register a gift of land made to the god
Traipurushadēva of the śāle at the agrahāra Tuṁbigi, by Mahāpradhāna, Hērisan-
dhivigrahi, Manevergaḍe, [Daṇḍa]nāyaka Ni[ṁ*]baṇa.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 11, Kshaya, Vaiśākha, puṇṇame,
Sunday. The tithi fell not on Sunday but on Friday, May 1, A.D. 1086.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(B.K. No. 122 of 1926-27).
KURTAKŌṬI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF VIRŪPĀKSHA.
inscription.p:
This inscription of the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) describes
at length the Two-Hundred Mahājanas of Kuṟitakūṇṭe, headed by the Ūroḍeya
Vāsudēva-Bhaṭṭa Sōmayāji, their trusted and valorous servant, the barber (nāvida)
Dāsa, who built the temple of Dāsēśvara, the members of the Mōriya family who
endowed the temple richly and the members of the Kōṇeya family. It registers a gift
of one paṇa on every dōṇi by the One-Thousand of Jaragara-Dōṇi, who belonged
to the Mōriya family and of oil by the Fifty Families of Telligas (oilmen), for worship,
lamp and offerings in the temple mentioned above. A gift of land is also stated to
have been made to the same temple by the Two-Hundred Mahājanas of Kuṟitakūṇṭe
and by the said Dāsa (Dāsimayya).

The record is dated Śaka 1009, Prabhava, Śrāvaṇa, amāvāsyā, Sunday, full solar
eclipse, vyatīpāta. The details regularly correspond to A.D. 1087, August 1, Sunday.

hiccagagētanintivar || ·amayarakētanuṁ negaḻda mācanuvaggada gujjagētanuṁ goravara
ciṇnanuṁ gavaṟerēvanu māyereyabbeyā-

ycanuṁ paramaparōpakāriyene saṁda negartteya huggedāsanuṁ sthiranene mikkanāgavaṟe mudda
nuvūrjjita naṁkimayyanu ||

gaṁgarasana bīranu vuttuṁgayaśōbhāgi yeḍavabasavayyanumā gaṁgaracāvuṇḍanumaṁ gaṁgā
dharabhaktanenipa mālarakalla || koḍagara kētaṁ

paḍavaḷarareyammaṁ mikkadāni gaḍiyarasiṁgaṁ toḍarddaragāḷaṁ keṁcaṁ poḍaviyo ḷutkriṣṭa
barmmikabbeyarāva || nāḍaṁkakā-

taṁ bīrakkāgaramenipa rāceyammana mallaṁ takkaṁ sāguḷagēsi jagakkoḷpaṁ mārppanenipa
naṇvara nāga || harivanajayadēvaṁ sa-

tpuruṣāgraṇi yenipa māramayyana ballaṁ sthiranā rakkasagētaṁ karuvara sāṁtayyaneseva bāri
ka bīca || dēhārada nāganu vutsā-

hada naleyenisi negaḻdhadēvagicaṭṭaṁ bāhubaḷadiṁde jīvipa sāhasiga rmmōriyānvaya
rkkēvaḷamē || mōriyakulatiḷaka rvvā-

ksārateyoḷ piriyarenipa sāsirvvaruvastārātidāsigaṁ vistāradi māḍida śivālaya kkā-
daradiṁ || nelanuṁ nēsaṟumu-

ḷḷina valaghubaḷaṟdōṇidōṇigaḷgoppaṇamaṁ sale varṣa[ṁ]prati koṭṭarppalariḻdoṁdāgi
māḍe tīraduduṁṭe || matta ||

talligacāvanuṁ tadanujaṁ sujanāgraṇi dēvapayyanuṁ sallalitāṁganāharatiyūrjjita-

nuḍiyadoḷḷida nārppina hayveseṭṭiyuṁ pollame tannalillenipa kōṇeyavaṁśaja nāyta-
varmanuṁ || kārasa holla-

nuṁ negardda vīraśikhāmaṇi kannamayyanuṁ bhāratamallaniṁdadhikarappa negartteya kōṇeyā-
nvayar śrīramaṇīpri-

yaṁ subhaṭagētanu mantavaraṇna caṭṭanuṁ sūrajanastutaṁ sujanajakkanu maggada guṇḍama-
yyanuṁ || navilara barmmaṁ maruḷaṁ

bhuvanastutarenisu vayvatokkalu matyutsavadiṁ dāsana māḍida śivabhavana kkittareṇneyaṁ
soṁṭigeya || paramaparōpa-

kārada tavarmmane mōriyavaṁśajargge tāyi paramapadābjabhriṁga suradhēnu samastasuhri
jjanakke tāṁ duritavido-

re dēsigaradāyi piriduṁ guṇadāgaraṁgaḷeṁ diradabhivarṇnikuṁ janacayaṁ sale pōriya jakki
kabbeya || sakavarṣaṁ 1009

neya prabhava saṁvatsarada śrāvaṇa damāvāsye ·ādityavāraṁ sarvvagrāsisūryagrahaṇaṁ vya
tīpātada tātāḷika-

doḷ jaragaradōṇisāsirvvaruṁ telligarayvatokkaluṁ śrīmatmābalēśvaradēvarapratibaddha
dakṣiṇasōmanāthaṁ

nāvidadāsayyana māḍida dāsēśvara dēvaraṁgabhōga nivēdyanandādīvigegavācaṁdrārkkatā-

madagrahāraṁ kuṟitakūṁṭeyūroḍeyapramukha vaśēṣamahājana vinnūrvvaru vūriṁ teṁka
muḻguṁdada

dāriyiṁ paḍuva kommarājana śatradekeyya teṁkaṇa poreya bhagavatīdēviya keyya paḍuvaṇa

ṭṭa mattar 5 nāvidadāsimayyaṁ tanna sarvvamānyaṁ baḍagavāgilamaneyo ḷīśānyada
gōṁṭinoḷ dēva raṁgabhōga nivēdya-

kke koṭṭa kaiyeraḍu yintī dharmmamanārorvva rkkādu naḍasidaravargge vāraṇāsi
kurukṣētraṁ prayāge yarggya-

tīrtthaṁ gaṁgāsāgarameṁba puṇyatīrtthaṁgaḷoḷ sāsirakavileyaṁ kōḍukoḷagumaṁ paṁca
ratsaṁgaḷoḷ kaṭṭisi mahā-

ritvija[rgga] ḷgubhayamukhigoṭṭa phaḷamakku madanaḷidaninitumanaḷidu narakaṁbokka paṁcama
hāpātakanakkuṁ ||

svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēta vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvvarṣasahasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē
krimiḥ || svaṁ dātuṁ

sumahacchakyaṁ duḥkhamanyasya sāḷanaṁ dānaṁ vā pāḷavaṁ vēti dānācchrēyōnupāḷanaṁ ||
parahita
rinnūrvvara padasarasīruhāmōdamadhukaraṁ śivabhaktaṁ karavatiśayamāgire vistaradiṁ ku-
ḷakaraṇi mābaḷāryyaṁ baredaṁ ||

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(B.K. No. 124 of 1926-27.)
KURTAKŌṬI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB LYING ON THE TANK-BUND.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI).
It praises the great qualities of Beḷkeyar-Eṟega, a member of the Two-Hundred
Mahājanas of the agrahāra Kuṟitakūṇṭe, who built the temples of Vishṇu and
Sarasvatī at Ermeganūr Kuṟipaṭṭi and a big well and a tank at Siriguppe.
It records the gift of land made by him, by purchase from the Mahājanas, to the
temple of Eṟe-Vishṇu Kēśavadēva, built by him.

The record is dated Śaka 1010, Prabhava, Śrāvaṇa, amāvāsyā, Sunday, solar
eclipse. The details regularly correspond to A.D. 1087, August 1, Sunday. This
is dated on the same day as No. 133 (No. 122 of 1926-27) above, though the Śaka
year there is cited as 1009.

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(B.K. No. 35 of 1928-29.)
ARASIBĪḌI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB STANDING ON A HILL TO THE RIGHT OF THE JAIN BASTI.
inscription.p:
This Kannaḍa inscription of the time of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
refers to his officer Mahāsāmantādhipati, Mahāprachaṇḍa-Daṇḍanāyaka, Mahāsēnā-
dhipati, [A]vararasa, who was governing over Kisukāḍu Seventy and Kariviḍi Thirty.
It registers a gift of land and house-sites made by the chief, with the consent of the
king, to the Forty-Two learned Mahājanas of the Brahmapurī, at the capital Vikrama-
pura.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 12, Prabhava, Śrāvaṇa, amāvāsyā,
Sunday, solar eclipse. The cyclic year, month, etc., are the same as those in the
foregoing record and correspond to A.D. 1087, August 1, Sunday.

mattribhuvana-

malladēvara vijayarājya muttarōttarābhivṛddhipravarddhamānamācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ

cāṁjanēyaṁ mahālakṣmī-

maṁ karivi-

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(B.K. No. 85 of 1927-28.)
ĀLŪR, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE VĪRABHADRADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription which is in Sanskrit and Kannaḍa belongs to the reign of
Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI). It begins with a formal preamble and gives
a long genealogy of the dynasty in the style of the copper-plate grants. This intro-
ductory portion very largely agrees with that of the Nīlgunda Plates of this king
(Ep. Ind. Vol. XII, pp. 142 ff.). It registers the gift of land, house-sites, etc., made by
the king to his servant (leṅka) Mahādēvayya-Nāyaka for the benefit of the temple of
Tripurushadēva built by the latter at Māḷad-Ālūru when the former was in his
military camp (vijaya-skandhāvāra) of Rājāśraya (See S.I.I. IX, No. 158, l. 60).
The Two-Hundred Mahājanas of the village, which was an agrahāra in Māsavāḍi
One-Hundred and Forty, received from this Mahādēvayya-Nāyaka, 130 Lokkigadyāṇas
and gave in exchange 200 mattar of land, etc., for the purpose of the gift. The
grant was made during the regime of Mahāpradhāna, Hērilāḷa-Kannaḍa-sandhivigrahi,
Daṇḍanāyaka Raviyaṇa-Bhaṭṭa. It may be noted that ‘Rājāśraya’ was the title of
the Western Chālukya king Vinayāditya (Bomb. Gaz., Vol. I, pt. 2, p. 368).

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 16, Prajāpati, Pushya, śu. dvādaśī
Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, which regularly corresponds to A.D. 1091,
December 25, Thursday.

rtti-

kēyavaraprasādalabdhamayūracchakuntadhvajānāṁ bhagavannārāyaṇaprasādāsādita vara-
varāhalāṁcchanēkasākṣaṇavaśīkritārātirājamaṇḍaḷānāṁ samastabhuva-

nāśrayaṁ sarvvalōkāśraya viṣṇuvarddhanaṁ vijayādityādiviśēṣanāmnāṁ rājaratā
yudpavabhūtiḥ || kavaḷitanaḷalakṣmīrddurjjayōditvaharī vihatapṛthvīkaḍaṁbāḍaṁ-
barō mā-

ryyanirjjitanijabhujabaḷabhūmnōtpāṭayanu rāṣṭrakūṭānu śrīḷitakaḷachuriśrīrasti
cāḷukyavaṁśaḥ | tajjōṣu rājyamanuśālya gatēṣu rājasvēkōnnaṣaṣṭi
gaṇanēṣu

purādhyayōdhyaṁ tadvaṁśajāstadanu śōḍaśabhūmipāḷāḥ kṣmādakṣiṇāpathajiṣāṁ vica
rāṁbubhūvu duṣṭāvaṣṭabudhāyā katipayapurṣāntarāntaritāyāṁ cāḷukyaku-

ḷapadi bhūyaścāḷukyavaṁśayēva || kanda || kīrttilatāṁkurasya kamaḷaṁ lakṣmī
nivāsāspadaṁ vajraṁ vairimahībhṛtāṁ pratinidhirddēvasya daityedruhaḥ rājāsīṁ jaya
siṁha-

bhūpativallabha ·iti khyātaścaritre[ni]jairyya rajē ciramādirājacaritōtkaṇṭhauḥ prajānāṁ
hranu || yō rāṣṭrakūṭa kulamindu ·iti prasiddha kṛṣṇāhvayasya

sutamaṣṭaśatēbhasainyā nijjitya dagdhanṛpapaṁcaśatē bhabhāra bhūyaścaḷukyakulavallabha
rājalakṣmīṁ || caṭuḷariputuragapaṭubhaṭakaraṭighaṭakōṭighaṭitaraṇa-

rāgaḥ sukṛtaharacaraṇarāgaḥ tanayōbhūttasya raṇarāgaḥ || tattanaya[ḥ] polakēsi nisūdana
samō bhavadrājā vatāpipurivarapatirakaḷitakhaḷakaḷikaḷekakaḷaḥ || kanda ||

vayamapi polikēsikṣmāpatiṁ varṇnāntaḥ puḷakakaḷitadēhāḥ paśyatādyāpi santaḥ sa hi
turagagajēndradgrāmasāraṁ sahaśradvayaparimitanṛtvigyācakārāśvamēdhē || tatta
nayā

naḷaniḷayaviḷōpirmauryyaniryyāṇāhētuḥ prathitaprithukadaṁbastaṁbhabhērīkuṭhāraṁ
bhuvanabhavanabhagā purāṇāraṁbhabhārabyavasitaśitakīrtti kīrttivarmmā nripōbhūt |
tasyā-

nujaḥ || sarvvadvīpākramaṇavayasā yasya nausētubandhīrullaṁghyābdhivyathitaprithunā
rēvatīdvīpalōpānu rājyaśrīṇāṁ haṭhapatirabhūdyaśca kāḷaccurīṇāṁ vabhrē
bhūmissa-

hasa sakalai rmmaṁgaḷairmmaṁgaḷīśaḥ || jyēṣṭhabhrātuḥ satisutavarapyarbhakatvādaśakti yasmi
nnātvārndha kritahitarammoṁgaḷīśa prithibyāṁ tasminu pratyānēnapi padadharmmāhi
yūni sa-

tyāśrayāsau cāḷukyānāṁ ka ·iva hi pathōdharmya taprabhyavēta || jīturdiśāṁ vijita
hariṣamahunripasya dāturmmanōrathaśatādhikamarthayadbhya satyādisarvvaguṇa-

ratna gaṇākarasya satyāśrayatvamupalakṣaṇamēva yasya || ·adhamarīkritadigvaḷayōrddi
tadviḍamarīpatigītamahāyaśāḥ mriḍamatiddabindamanasōdvaha nēḍamatikṣitī-
śōjani-

tōtsātaṁ || sutastadīyō guṇaratnamauḷī bhūvallabhō[bhū*]dbhujavīryyaśāḷī
ādityavarmmōrjjitapuṇyakarmmā tējōbhi rādityasamānadharmmā || tatsutō
vikramādityō vikra-

makrānta bhūtaḷaḥ tathāpi yuddhamallākhyō yuddhē yamasamā nripāḥ || tajjanmā
vijayādityō vīrānēkāṁgasaṁgaraiḥ caturṇnamaṇḍaḷānāmapyuṁjayadvijayōpa-
mān || ©

tadbhavō vikramādityaḥ kīrttivarmmā tadātmajaḥ yēnaścāḷukyarājyaśrīrantarāyiṇya
bhūdbhuviḥ || vikramādityabhūpāḷabhrāntā bhīmaparākrama tatsūnuḥ kīrtti-
varmmā-

bhūnmṛtprāsārdditadurjjana || taiḷabhūpa(ḥ)statō jātō vikramādityabhūpatiḥ
tatsūnurabhavattasmāt bhīmarājōribhīkaraḥ || taiḷabhū ·ajjaṇāryyastutō jajñē
yaduvaṁśa-

syaśriyaṁ svakāṁ prāpaya(ṁ)nniravaṁśasvaṁ savajraṁ kriṣṇanandanā || ·abhavastayōsta-
nujō vibhavavibhāsivirōdhividhvaṁsi tējōvijitādityaḥ satyadhanō vikramādityaḥ ||

bēgīśavaṁśatiḷakā lakṣmaṇārājasya naṁdanānnu śīḷāṁ boṁthādēvīṁ vidhivatu
pariṇinnē vikramādityaḥ || sutamiva vasudēvāddēvakī vāsudēvaṁ guhamiva
girijāmirddē

vamarddhēṁdumauḷēḥ ·ajanayadatha boṁthādēbyataḥ staiḷabhūpaḥ vibhavavijitaśakraṁ vikra
mādityanāmnāṁ || karikuṁbhikuṁbhabhēdanaripudurggakavāṭabhaṁjanaprabhṟitisahajabaḷasya
harēriva bā-

ḷakṛḍābhavadyasya || kiṁca rāṣṭrakūṭakuḷarājya saṁbuddhābubhā || ·ajjityācaraṇa
vivapra caḷitau sākṣātkadēkramataḥ krūrau baddhaśarīrakau gurujanarddrōhappra-
rōhā viva kāḷā-

tṁḍitarāṣṭrakūṭakakuḷaśīvalli jātāṁkurau lōṇayēna sukhēna karkkara raṇa
staṁbhaṁ raṇiprāṁgaṇē || ·itthaṁ purā ditisutēriva bhūtadhātrīṁ yō rāṣṭrakūṭa
kuṭiḷai

rggamitāmadhastātu ·uddhritya mādhava ·ivādivarāharūpō vabhrē caḷukyakulavallabha
rājalakṣmīṁ || bhaṇmaharaṭṭādabhavadbhūpāḷā(r)ddrā(r)ṣṭrakūṭakuḷatiḷakātu
lakṣmīriva śaḷi-

ḷanidhē śrījākabbāhvayā kanyā || cāḷukyavaṁśāvarabhānmāḷī śrītailabhūpāḷa ·u
pāyataināṁ tayōśca lōkābhyudayāya yōgaḥ sacandrikācandramasō

rivāsīt || śrītaiḷabhūmipāḷāt śrījākabbā samajījanatō śrīmatsatyāśraya
skaṁdamaṁbikātryiyambikādiva || tasyānujaśśrī daśavarmmanāmā tadvallabhā

bhāgyavetīti dēvī tayōrabhūdvikramaśīḷaśāḷī śrīvikramādityanripastanūjaḥ || ·asau
nijajyēṣṭa bitaṟparōkṣaṁ vabhārā nidritāṁ dharitrīṁ

bhujēna kēyūralatāmivō(r)cyairvvidāritārātikada(ṁ)mbakēna || tadanu tasyānuja
hiṁsadāvanastaṁ paṭu(ṭu)vikramādyō madāndhagandhēbhaghaṭāvipāṭī dharōrjjita-
prassurita(ḥ)prabhāvō ra-

rāja yoṁśau jayasi[ṁ*]harājaḥ || tataḥ pratāpa(ḥ)jvaḷanaprabhāvanirmmūḷanirddagdha
virōdhidantaḥ || tasyātmajaḥ pāyitā dharāyā śrīmānabhūdāhavamalladēvaḥ ||
tasyātma-

jāyatajagajjavitapramōdaśriṁgāravīrarasikaḥ kavilōkakāntaḥ kāntāvilōḷavaya
ḷacārucandraścāḷukyavaṁśatiḷakō bhuvanaykamallaḥ tadanu(ḥ)

tasyānujaḥ || ·āsījaḥkalitakamaḷōllāsanaḥ prauḍhapādaḥ sparṣāduccaiśriyamava
bhracchēka rāṇādadhā dhvāntabhrāntidadhadiva driśōraṁjanaṁ vairivīsmārākṣī
ṇāṁ mmau-

hurapaharanvikramādityadēvaḥ bhūvīrannamitaphaṇīśvaraṁ bhujābhyāṁ vibhrāṇaṁ paṭaha
palāyataṁ kṣitīśaḥ yaścēccairapahritanākiśākhiḷīḷaḥ prakhyāta-

stṛbhuvanamalla ·ityudāraḥ || yātōnyē[ṣṭa]ṁ janakajanitāvallabhōdāralakṣmībhrātrā
sārddhaṁ harivaḷayutaṁ svāsamitrātmajēna tīrē sinmayirvvahimukhabhayāddai-

tya vaibhīṣaṇaśrīdhāmnānēmē draviḷapatinā yaśca cāḷukyarāmaḥ || sarvvēśāṁ vija
yaprayāṇasamaya jātākhiḷōrvvīpada prasthānēva mahīmatītya vi-

maḷā yatkīrttirabdhiṁgatāḥ prēśyastē vijayaśriyā paravaśā dūrīkarō tyaiśumā
mityākhyātumivārṇnavasthitijuṣaḥ saurēstriḷōkīgurōḥ || sa tu śrī-

prithvīvallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvaraṁ paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ
cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmatribhuvanamalladēvaḥ ku[śa]ḷī sarvvānēva yathāsaṁba-

ddhyamānyakānu rāṣṭrapati viśeyapati grāmakūṭakāyuktakaniyuktakādhikārika
mahattarādīnsamādisatyastuvaḥ saṁviditaṁ yadhāsmābhiḥ || rājāvaḷi-

ssamāptaḥ || ·ālōlajaladhivēṣṭita bhūlōkadoḷeseva māḷadālūroḷu tannālayadoḷu
sukhamiḷpaṁ līleye tauduṁba periya māḷaṁkryāḷa || tatsuta || ·ācāraṁ

pēḷvaḍe manvācāraṁ sakaḷavidye tanagadu hṛdyaṁ vācakadoḷvācaspati dēceyabhaṭṭa
lalāṭapaṭṭaṁ dvijaroḷu || ·ātana kulavadhu vinayōpēte mahāsati mahāpatibra-

te puruśaprītiyo[ḷ ne]gaḷvaḷendu mahītaḷavā cākaṇabbeyaṁ pogaḷadarāru || tatsuta ||
ātage samastaguṇōpētaṁge mahānbhāvage maga prītiyoḷe

puṭṭidaṁ raghujitasutapratimanenipa mahādēvayyaṁ || guṇada tavarmane guṇanidhi guṇā-
bdhiguṇamēruvenisi saṁdirṛda mahāguṇanidhi mahadēvayyaṁ triṇe-

yanapādābjabhṛṁga vāḷdana siṁga || ·ātaṁ samastaguṇanidhānaṁ leṁkapradhānaṁ leṁkamāṇi
kyaṁ nīticāṇākyaṁ budhajanasarōjinīrājahaṁsaṁ sarasvatīkarṇnāvataṁsaṁ

śrīvasiṣṭagōtrasamuddharaṇaṁ bandhucintāmaṇi vivēkacūḍāmaṇi śrīmatribhuvanamalla
dēvara leṁkaṁ mahādēvayyanāyakargge || svasti samastapraśastisa-

hitaṁ śrīmanmahāpradhāna hērilāḷakannaḍasandhivigrahi daṇḍanāyakaṁ raviyaṇabhaṭṭa
rāḷkeyalu || cāḷukyavikramavarṣē śōḍaśē prajāpati saṁva-

tsarē pravarttamānē tadantarggata puśya śuddha dvādasyāṁ brihaspativārē ·uttarāyaṇa
saṁkramaṇaparvvaṇi bahūni mahādānāni datvā taddakṣiṇakālē rājā-

śrayasamāvāsitavijayaskandhāvārē leṁka mādiyaṇē[na] māḷadālūrugrāmē kārita
tripurṣadēvāyatana khaṇḍasphuṭita nava • • karmmārtthaṁ pu(ḥ)-

ḻpārghyāgandhadhūpapanivēdyādyarccanārtthaṁ gītavādyanrityārtthaṁ ca māsavāḍi
catvāriṁśadadhikaśatamadhyē ·agrahāraṁ mmāḷadālūru grāmē ·uttaradiśāyāṁ

koḷaṁpe·itatkācca bhūkṣētrē bhūmivarttanaṁ śatadvayantathā baṭṭakeṟegērimadhyē griha-
nivēśanāni catvāri baṇaṁbevoṭṭu • • • • tathā • • • • • tatusama-

staṁ sarvvanamasyānāṁ śāsanīkritya dattaṁ yadyapi rājadattantathāpi || svasti yama-
niyamasvādhyāyadhyānadhāraṇamaunānuṣṭhā • • • rāyajapasamādhiśīḷaguṇa
saṁpa-

nnarappa śrīmadagrahāra[ṁ]mmāḷadālūra ·ūroḍeyapramukha mahājanavinnūrvaruṁ
grāmakāryyanimittavāgi pa[sā]yitaṁ leṁka surigeya mahādēvayya-

nāyakara kaiyyalu koṁḍa lokkiya parivarttakadaccina poṁgadyāṇaṁ nūṟamūvattu ·aṁka-
doḷ poṁgadyāṇa 130 kka koṭṭa bhūmiya staḷa ·iṭṭageya polada mēre-

yiṁ banniyakeṟeya sthaḷadalu koṭṭa keyi mattaru 200 kkaṁ sīme baḍagalu ·allamēśvara
dēvara keyi ·alliṁ paḍuvalu biṭṭa vrittiya keyi ·aliṁ paḍuvalu haṭṭa-

gāṟarakeyi paḍuvaṇa sīme ·aṁgaḍiya dāvaṇnana satrakke biṭṭakeyyiṁ mūḍalu tikimayyana
satrada keyyiṁ paḍuvalu seṭṭigeyyiṁ baḍagalu piriyasatra-

da keyyiṁ baḍagalu baṭṭakeṟagēriyalekkayyamane nālku baṇaṁbe voṭṭeraḍu tōṭa ·anta-
nitakkaṁ ·innūrvvaru sarvvabādhāparihāravāgi pratipāḷisuvaru || śrī saraśvatye
namaḥ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(B.K. No. 75 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE KALLĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and states that while (the goddess) Mahālakshmīdēvī of Kollāpura was presiding
over and protecting Soṟaṭavura, Telliga Jakkiyabbe bought some land from the
Two-Hundred Mahājanas of the place and dedicated it for a satra for feeding
the Brāhmaṇas. The same lady is stated, in a supplement, to have made a similar
gift on a second occasion.

The record contains two dates. The first is Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 16,
Prajāpati, Pushya, śu. 12, Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, vyatīpāta. This is
identical with the date of the previous record and corresponds to A.D. 1091,
December 25, Thursday. The second date is Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 17, Āṅgira,
Paushya, śu. 10, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, vyatīpāta. The tithi and week-
day agree and fall on A.D. 1092, December 26, Sunday; but the saṅkrānti occurred
on the previous Friday, i.e., December 24.
The record was composed by Mādhava-Bhaṭṭa.

sāsirbbarvvēdapāragarggubhayamukhigoṭṭa phaḷamakku © ·idanaḷidaninitumanaḷi-

patihitaprakhyāte sāyabbegaṁ kṣameyaṁ tāḷdida jakkiyabbe śubha māgalpu-

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(B.K. No. 215 of 1928-29.)
BEḶVAṬIGI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE RĀMALIṄGADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This highly damaged inscription of the time of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) begins with a brief narration of the genealogy of the king, from
Tailapa (Taila II) and registers a gift of land, made by Bairumbara Mallimayya, (or
Mallaṇa) to the temples of Bairumbēśvara and Sarasvatī, built by him at Beḷḷiṭṭage.
The land was purchased by him from the Twelve Gāvuṇḍas of the village headed
by the Ūroḍeya and was situated in the locality called Daṇḍuḷabāgu.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 18, Śrīmukha, Paushya, śu. 11, Sunday,
vyatīpāta, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa. The tithi quoted fell not on Sunday but on
Saturday and occurred a week after the Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, i.e., on Saturday,
Pushya śu. 4 = December 24, A.D. 1093.

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(B.K. No. 90 of 1929-30.)
HEBBĀḶ, BĀGEVĀḌI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB STANDING NEAR THE MALLIKĀRJUNA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and is interesting on account of the mention of the king's son and
crown prince Yuvarāja Mallikārjunadēva, who was governing over the Tardavāḍi
Thousand province as tribhōg-ābhyantara-siddhi. It registers a gift of land made
by the prince, at the request of his subordinate Mahāsāmantādhipati, Daṇḍanāyaka,
Bhīvaṇayya, to the temple of the goddess Sarasvatī. The gift was made into the
hands of Chandraśēkhara Paṇḍita, the āchārya of the maṭha in the temple of
Svayaṁbhu Dakshiṇa-Sōmanātha at Perbbāḷ, the chief among the Twelve (villages).

The inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 20, Yuva, Paushya, ba. 11,
Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, which corresponds to A.D. 1095, December 24,
Monday, on which day the saṅkrānti actually commenced.

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(B.K. No. 74 of 1927-28.)
DŌṆI, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the time of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and records the gift of a garden made by Sōvi-seṭṭi to Chārukīrtti-Paṇḍita, the
disciple of Munichandra Traividya-Bhaṭṭāraka, of the Yāpunīya-saṅgha and
Vṛikshamūla-gaṇa, for the benefit of the basadi, built by him, evidently at
Drōṇāpura. The gift was made in the presence of the Sixty Mahājanas of the
great agrahāra Drōṇāpura headed by the Ūroḍeya and the several Seṭṭis of the
place.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 20, [Yuva], Phālguṇa, śu. 13,
Thursday. In Yuva, Phālguṇa, śu. 13 fell on Friday commencing at ·13 and
corresponded to Friday, February 8, A.D. 1096. In the next year Dhātu, however,
the details correspond to A.D. 1097, February 26, Thursday; the tithi commenced
at ·11.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(B.K. No. 105 of 1929-30).
MUTTIGI, BĀGEVĀḌI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB NEAR THE RUDRAGAṆṬI-RĀMALIṄGA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) who is stated to have then been ruling from his nelevīḍu at Jayantīpura. It
records a gift of land and garden made by Tikeya Shaṇaṅgi to the god Tikēś-
varadēva at Muttage, an uttamad-agrahāra, and the chief village in the kampaṇa
of Muttage Thirty, a sub-division of Taddavāḍi Thousand.

The record is dated the year 21 of the king's reign, Īśvara, Pushya, śu. 5,
Thursday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. Pushya śu. 5 fell on Friday, December 11, A.D.
1097, but the saṅkrānti occurred on Pushya ba. 3, Thursday, December 24, A.D.
1097.

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(B.K. No. 150 of 1928-29).
KAṬGĒRI, BĀDĀMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB LYING IN THE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription is of the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI), who was ruling from Kalyāṇa and registers a gift of money made by his
queen Abhinava-Sarasvatī Chandaladēvī, for the Ṛigvēda-khaṇḍikā and the Śāstra-
khaṇḍikā. The Mahājanas of the agrahāra Kaḍakeṟe are mentioned.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 21, Īśvara, • • • • pañchamī,
Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, vyatīpāta, In Īśvara Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa occur-
red on Pushya ba. 3, Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1097, December 24. But in
the previous year, i.e., Dhātṛi, Pushya śu. pañchamī fell on Monday, December 22nd,
A.D. 1096, though the saṅkrānti occurred two days, later i.e., Wednesday, December
24, A.D. 1096.

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(B.K. No. 203 of 1928-29).
AṆṆIGĒRI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE MAILĀRAPPA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This much damaged inscription of the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI) seems to register a gift of money made by Mahāmātya, Rājādhyaksha
Baladēvayya Nāyaka, of the Ātrēya gōtra. The money was to be utilised for
dakshiṇā to Brāhmaṇas on the full-moon day of Śrāvaṇa.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 22, Īśvara, Śrāvaṇa, puṇṇame, Sunday,
saṅkramaṇa, lunar eclipse. The tithi and week-day agree with Sunday, July 26,
A.D. 1097, on which day pūrṇimā commenced, but the Siṁha-saṅkramaṇa occurred
on the next day. There was no lunar eclipse on this day. But in the previous
year Dhātṛi, there was a lunar eclipse on Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15; but the week day-was
Wednesday.

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(B.K. No. 65 of 1927-28).
DAMBAḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON THE WALL TO THE LEFT OF ENTRANCE INTO THE JAINA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and records the gift of oil made by the Fifty Families of oilmen (Telligar), for
a perpetual lamp in the Bauddha-vihāra built by Saṅgava-Seṭṭi.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 23, Bahudhānya, Āshāḍha, ba.
ēkādaśī, Sunday, saṅkramaṇa. The corresponding English date is A.D. 1098, June
27, Sunday, but the Karkaṭaka, i.e., Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkramaṇa occurred on the pre-
vious day.

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1926-27.)
SORAṬŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON ONE OF THE BEAMS BUILT INTO A WELL CALLED PANTARA-BHĀVI.
inscription.p:
This damaged and partly built-in inscription belongs to the reign of
Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and registers gifts of certain toll-incomes
made to the temple of Grāmēśvaradēva by the Two-Hundred Mahājanas headed by
the Ūroḍeya, the Ugura Three-Hundred, the Fifty Families of oilmen, the toll-officers,
etc. Kēdāraśakti Paṇḍita, disciple of Bōḷa-Paṇḍita of Lokkiguṇḍi, received the gift.
Mention is also made of the gift of the baṇṭa-paṇa by the Baṇṭa One-Thousand.

The date of the record is [Chāḷukya-Vikrama] year 23, Bahudhānya (= A.D.
1098-99), further details of which are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(B.K. No. 132 of 1926-27.)
KŌṬAVUMACHIGI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE SŌMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription of the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
praises at length the glory of the king's subordinate, Mahāsāmantādhipati, Mahā-
pradhāna, Bāṇasavergaḍe, Anantapāḷarasa, who was the governor of Beḷvala Three-
Hundred and Purigeṟe Three-Hundred. Mention is specially made of his large
bounties for feeding and for educational purposes. The immediate purpose of the
record is not clear; but probably it was a gift by the chief himself to the god
Svayaṁbhū Sōmēśvara. The record was composed by the poet Chandra-Bhaṭṭa.

The inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 2 • • • • Pushya, śu. [1]1,
Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṅkrānti. The details agree in Śaka 1021, Pramāthin, which
was Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 23. The corresponding English date would be A.D. 1099,
December 25, Sunday; f.d.t. ·27.

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(B.K. No. 151 of 1926-27.)
CHIKKAHANDIGŌḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE HANUMĀN TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and commences with the praise of the king's general, Anantapāla Daṇḍādhīśa who is
stated to have been ruling over the Two-Six-hundred (province), Banavāse-nāḍu,
Kūṇḍi-nāḍu and Baḷayavaṭṭaṇa. It then proceeds to mention his younger brothers
Nāraṇadēva, Vāvaṇa, and Lakkaṇa and their officer Mādhava who was administering
the Beḷvala country. It mentions the charities founded by Mādhava at Anyataṭāka
(Aṇṇigeri). It then records the foundation of a tank and a temple of Mādhavēśvara
at Kiṟiya Pandigoḷa (Chikkahandigōḷ) by Mādhava's son Pratikaṇṭha Chanda and
also gifts of lands and house-site made by him to the temple, after purchasing them
from the Sixty-Four Mahājanas of the place. The epigraph was composed by Malla
and engraved by Rūvāri Kētōja.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 24, Pramādin, Pushya, ba. 8,
Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. The date is irregular. The Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti
occurred on Pushya śu. 10, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1099, December 25,
Sunday, ·22.

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(B.K. No. 22 of 1927-28.)
HANAMANHĀḶ, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE LYING IN SURVEY No. 18.
inscription.p:
This slightly damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) who was ruling from his nelevīḍu at Kalyāṇapura. It registers
certain contributions in kind on (the sale of) betel leaves to a certain Siṅga Gāvuṇḍa of
Āluṁgeṟe in Kisukāḍu Seventy for his services to the One Thousand (Community of)
Tambuligas made by several Seṭṭis and others in the presence of (the represent-
atives of) the Five-Hundred Svāmis, Eighteen Paṭṭaṇas, Thirty-two Vēḷāpuras
Sixty-four Ghaṭikāsthānas, Sixty-four Yōgapīṭhas, the Sixteen of the Eight Nāḍus,
Gavaṟe, Gātrigas, Bīras and Bīravaṇigas.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 24, Pramādin, Paushya, śu. 10,
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, Sunday. These details regularly correspond to A.D. 1099,
December 25, Sunday; ·22.

lyāṇapurada nelevīḍinoḷu sukhasaṁkhathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁge-

·eṁṭunāḍa padinaṟuvaruṁ gavaṟeyuṁ gātrigaruṁ bīraruṁ bīravaṇiga-

lidēvaseṭṭivuṁ hāvariya hicciseṭṭi tiḷivaḷḷiya kaliyammaseṭṭi-

tucciṁgeṟeya holliseṭṭi huḷḷamana sōviseṭṭi [gā]javūra ·āriya padma-

kāḍeppattaṟa baḷiya bāḍada ·āluṁgeṟeya siṁgagāvuṁṇḍaṁ taṁbuligasā-
sirvvarartthaṁ tūṟaharu koṇḍaḍā yartthaṁ siṁgagāvuṁṇḍa nikkisi taṁda-
[ḍe] sāsirvvaru miḻdu siṁgagavuṁḍaṁge mecci koṭṭa sāsanameṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(B.K. No. 190 of 1926-27.)
NARĒGAL, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE STANDING INSIDE A RUINED WELL CALLED HIRĒBHĀVI.
inscription.p:
The inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and introduces his subordinate Mahāpradhāna, Bāṇasavergaḍe, Daṇḍanāyaka
Anantapāḷayya as the governor of Beḷvala Three-hundred and Purigeṟe Three-hundred
provinces. It states that Mārasiṅga Gāvuṇḍa, son of Mācha-Gāvuṇḍa and grandson
of Dēva-Gāvuṇḍa of Nareyaṅgal, who belonged to the Murttaga family, made a gift of
land and oil-mill for the benefit of the temple of Kalidēvasvāmin of Bāviyamaṭha
into the hands of the Sixteen, the Eighteen and the Pañchamaṭhasthāna.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 25, Vikrama, Mārgaśira, puṇṇami,
Sunday, lunar eclipse. The details regularly correspond to Sunday, 18th November,
A.D. 1100, on which day there occurred a lunar eclipse.

bhamūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē ||

nāḷuttamiralu | kanda || dhareyo • • riya vadanakkire no • • le-

dinadaṁdu bāviyamaṭheda śrī kalidēvasvāmidēvara ·aṁga-
bhōgakke tanna baḷada mūḍaṇa poladoḷ kukkanūra baṭṭeyiṁ baḍagalaḍa-
vūra geyi baṭṭeyiṁ teṁka kuḷḷiya viṭṭaṁ mūḍa liṁgamuṁ kavile-
yuṁ tapōdhanaruṁ berasu māḍida nālkuṁ gōṁṭina taḍigallaguḍḍeye mēre-
yāga laruvaṇa 1 māge biṭṭa bhūmi mattarirppattanālkuma nalliya padi-
naṟuvaruṁ padineṇbaruṁ paṁcamaṭasthānamuṁ mūṟu modalu mirddācaṁ-
drārkasthāyivara vā dharmmama niṁbāgire pratipālisuvar mattaṁ
mārasiṁgagāvuṇḍaṁ dēvargge biṭṭa nelaseṇa teṁkaṇa bhāgada mēreyiṁ baḍaga ka-

yaṁ dēvargge biṭṭa tōṭaṁ jakkiliya dāriyiṁ paḍuva 1 mattaṁ ·arasiya bā-

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(B.K. No. 5 of 1926-27.)
GADAG, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE PRĀKĀRA WALL OF THE TRIKŪṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the regin of Tribhuvanamalladēva and registers
the gift of some cesses made to the god Sōmanātha (in the temple) of Svayaṁbhū
Trikūṭēśvaradēva of Gaḻdugu, an agrahāra in Beḷvala-nāḍu, by Mahāpradhāna
Achchupannāyadadhishṭhāyaka Daṇḍanāyaka Mādhava-Bhaṭṭa, a subordinate of
Mahāpradhāna Bhīvaṇayya, who was governing Palasige Twelve-Thousand (province).
and was in charge of the achchu-pannāya revenue of the Seven-and-a-half-lakh
(country). Bhīvaṇayya is stated to be the chief of the Sāvāsis (Sāvāsigaḷ-adhish-
ṭhāyaka). The grant was made after washing the feet of Kriyāśakti-Paṇḍita,
āchārya of the maṭha attached to the temple. The record is dated Chāḷukya-
Vikrama year 27, Chitrabhānu, Chaitra, śu. 1, Sunday. The date seems to be irregular.
If śu. is a mistake for ba. the equivalent would be A.D. 1102, April 6, Sunday.

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(B.K. No. 8 of 1927-28.)
BEḶVAṆKI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING CLOSE TO THE TEMPLE OF KALAMĒŚVARA.
inscription.p:
This slightly damaged inscription of the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikram-
āditya VI) records the gift of land made by Palageya Chāvuṇḍamayya and his wife
Sāntikabbe for the upkeep of the sabhā-maṇṭapa and the tank Muttalageṟe cons-
tructed by them, to the Eighty-Four Mahājanas of the agrahāra village Beḷvaṇige,
headed by the Ūroḍeya Vishṇu-Bhaṭṭōpādhyāya. The epigraph was engraved by Taila.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 27, Chitrabhānu, Pushya, puṇṇami,
Thursday. The date corresponds to A.D. 1102, December 25, Thursday, on which day
puṇṇami commenced at ·27.

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(B.K. No. 114 of 1926-27.)
HOSŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON TWO SLABS BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE TRIKŪṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This record refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and is
dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 27, Chitrabhānu, Māgha, śu. 11, Thursday, sankramaṇa.
The date does not seem to be regular. If, however, śu. 11 is a mistake for śu. 13, the
equivalent would be A.D. 1103, January 22, Thursday; but the saṅkramaṇa (Kuṁbha)
occurred on the following day. This inscription registers the grant of some plots of
land by Mahāprabhu Nāki-Gāvuṇḍa, who was administering Posavūru as ēkabhōga, for
worship and offerings in the temple of Morakēśvara constructed by him. The donor
whose genealogy is given, is stated to have belonged to the Mōraka-kula. The gift was
made after washing the feet of the teacher Jñānaśakti, while Sakaḷaprakāśa-Bhaṭṭāraka
was governing the Perggēri of Muḷgunda and Posavūru. Posavūru is stated to
to have been situated in Muḷugunda Twelve, a sub-division of Beḷvala-nāḍu and to
have been the property of the god Vīranārāyaṇadēva evidently at Muḷugunda. (See
B.K. No. 88 of 1926-27 below.) Other gifts to the same temple by the Ugara Three-
Hundred and the One-Hundred are also recorded.

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(B.K. No. 240 of 1926-27.)
SŪḌI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP AT A CORNER OF THE FRONT WALL OF THE HOUSE
OF SIDDAPPA ROṆ.
inscription.p:
This record refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and
registers a gift of gold by Uttavōja, the king's goldsmith, to god Kammaṭēśvara
of Sūṇḍi.

The epigraph is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 28, Subhānu, Uttarāyaṇa-
saṅkramaṇa = A.D. 1103, December 25, Friday (not verifiable).

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 455 of 1926).
KANAKŪR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR SET UP IN THE TEMPLE OF KANAKAMMA.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription in Sanskrit and Kannaḍa refers itself to the reign of
Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) who was ruling from his capital Viḷāsa[pura].
It registers the gift of the village Kanakāpura in Kundūr Five-Hundred for burning
incense in the temple of Sōmanāthadēva of the Saurāshṭra vishaya by Mahā-
maṇḍaḷēśvara Jayakēśidēva at the time of his marriage, under the direction of the
king. Jayakēśidēva is styled Koṁkaṇādhipati. Another gift to Vāmana-Bhaṭṭ-
ōpādhyāya, the Ūroḍeya of Kanakāpura, is mentioned in the earlier portion.
1 The inscription stops here.

Jayakēśidēva of this record is apparently the Kādaṁba prince Jayakēśin II, the
son-in law of Vikramāditya VI. This inscription would thus furnish the earliest date
for him.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year [27], Subhānu, Māgha śu, 13, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1104, January 11, Monday; f.d.t. 07.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(B.K. No. 181 of 1926-27.)
NARĒGAL, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE VILLAGE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
This inscription, which is slightly damaged, refers itself to the reign of
Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and is dated Śaka 1028, Tāraṇa, Kārttika,
amāvāsye, Thursday, solar eclipse. The date is irregular. The cyclic year Tāraṇa
corresponded to Śaka 1026 = A.D. 1104-05 and in that year Kārttika amāvāsye
did not fall on a Thursday; nor was there a solar eclipse on that day. The
epigraph registers the gift of land, house-site and oil-mill by Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara
Bammarasa for worship and for burning lamps in the temple of Brahmēśvara built
by him at Nareyaṅgal. The gift was made into the hands of Vigrēśvara Paṇḍita.
The genealogy of the Sinda chiefs of Erambarage is traced down to Permāḍi, the son
of Āchugi II and he is stated to have been governing Kīsukāḍu Seventy, Keḷavaḍi
(Three Hundred) and other districts. The date cited in the record, however, is inad-
missible for the Sinda chief Permāḍi. (Vide, Bomb. Gaz. Vol. I, pt. II, p. 575, f.n.
3 and Ep. Ind. Vol. XX, p. 114.)

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(B.K. No. 118 of 1926-27.)
KAṆVI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON THE WALL (LEFT OF ENTRANCE) OF THE ĪŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 30, Pārthiva, Pushya, śu. 2, Sunday,
corresponding to A.D. 1105, December 10, Sunday. It registers a gift by Ajji-
Gāvuṇḍa, who was administering Kaṇivūr, for the beatitude of his father Jayadēva
Gāvuṇḍa. A Śaiva teacher named Indraśivadēvāchāryya, who was the royal preceptor
(Rājaguru) and the head of the temple of Śōbhanēśvaradēva at Muḷugunda and
other Śiva temples in the Seven-and-a-half-lakh country is eulogised.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(B.K. No. 88 of 1926-27.)
MUḶGUND, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON THE DHVAJASTAṀBHA IN FRONT OF THE NĀRĀYAṆADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This damaged and incomplete inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvana-
malladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year [32], Sarvadhāri,
Jyēshṭha, amāvāsye, Wednesday, solar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1108, June 11,
Thursday, when there was a solar eclipse; the week day is probably an error.
It mentions Mahāpradhāna Sēnādhipati Daṇḍanāyaka Chidda and the temple of
Vīranārāyaṇadēva at the town of Muḷgunda.

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(B.K. No. 66 of 1926-27.)
YALI-SIRŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO A WALL OF THE BHŌGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 34, Virōdhi, Āśvayuja, ba. 5, Thursday,
corresponding to A.D. 1109, September 16, Thursday; the month was Adhika-Āśva-
yuja. It registers grants of land, gold and toll incomes, made by Eight Gāvuṇḍas,
the Sixty Families, the Five-Hundred Nakara and other guilds such as Dōṇi One-
Thousand, Ugura Three-Hundred, etc., to the temple of Bhōgēśvara of Eleya-Śrīpura.
Another gift made to the same temple by Mahāpradhāna Sāyipayya and the Mahāpra-
hāna, Karaṇada Nāgavarmayya is also recorded. A third gift is stated to have been
made by certain Seṭṭis to the gods Bhōgēśvara and Amṛitēśvara and the goddess
Sarasvatīdēvī. Bhūtarasa and Malidēvarasa of the Baisa-kula and residents of Toṟagale
are also stated to have made a gift of certain income to god Bhōgēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(B.K. No. 172 of 1926-27.)
ROṆ, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB LEANING AGAINST THE RIGHT WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF DRŌṆĀCHĀRYA.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) and is dated Śaka 1033, Khara, Kārttika, paurṇamāsye, Wednesday,
Vyatīpāta, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1111, October 18, Wednesday, when
there was a lunar eclipse. It registers the grant of gold and land made by Nāgavishṇu-
mayya of Haiṅgakula for lighting the sabhā-maṇḍapa and vakkhāṇa-śāle constructed
by-him, for the Sarasvatī-bhōjana on the occasion of Aindrōtsava, for teaching tarka
in the vakkhāṇa-śāle and for other charitable institutions.

inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1926.)
NIGADI, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE KALAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE ON THE TANK BUND.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the reign of Permāḍidēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and is dated the cyclic year Madhyama-Nandana, Vaiśākha, ba. Śivarātri, Monday.
The date appears to be irregular. Ba. 14 of Vaiśākha in the year Nandana began
on Friday, 26th April, 1112 A.D. In Khara (= Śaka 1033) however, the date
regularly corresponds to Monday, 8th May, 1111 A.D. The record registers a grant
of land and a maṭha made to Tējōrāśi-Paṇḍita by Chāvuṇḍa-Gāvuṇḍa of Nigunde
while Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Gūvaladēva of the Kadaṁba dynasty was governing
the Koṅkaṇa-maṇḍaḷa from his capital Gōve. Nigunde (modern Nigadi in the
Dharwar Taluk) is stated to have been situated in Mugunda Thirty, a subdivision
(kaṁpaṇa) of Mārajavāḍi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(B.K. No. 130 of 1926-27.)
KŌṬAVUMACHIGI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE ŚŌMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) and is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 37, Nandana, Pushya, śu. [4,
Tuesday], Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1112, December 24, Tuesday.
It mentions a certain Vāmanārya and the village Umachige.

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(B.K. No. 3 of 1930-31.)
DIṆḌAVĀR, BĀGĒVĀḌI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE LAKKAVVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 35, Nandana, Pushya, śu. 10, Sunday, Utta-
rāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, corresponding to A.D. 1112, December 29, Sunday; f.d.t. ·05. The
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, however, had occurred on the 24th December. The epigraph
registers a gift made for worship etc., to god Māḷēśvaradēva by Mahāmaṇḍa-
ḷēśvara [Bhī]shaṇadēvarasa, while he was administering the village Diṇḍāvura,
included in the six villages of Keṁpanakuḷi, situated in Tarddavāḍi-nāḍu. He is stated
to have been the subordinate of Yuvarāja Mallikārjunadēva, who was governing
the Tarddavāḍi Thousand, and an aṅkakāṟa of Kīrttirājadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(B.K. No. 219 of 1926-27.)
ABBIGĒRI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE LEANING AGAINST THE LEFT WALL OF THE VILLAGE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) and is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 37, Nandana, Phālguṇa,
amāvāsye, Sunday, solar eclipse, corresponding probably to A.D. 1113, March 19,
Wednesday, when there was a solar eclipse; the week-day seems to be an error. The
record revives a lapsed grant of land made by Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Ācharasa at
the request of Āycharāja, the pergaḍe of Abheyageṟe, the Eight Gāvuṇḍas, the Sixty
Families and others to the tank (called) Dēviṁgeṟe. Abheyageṟe is stated to have been
situated in Nareyaṁgal Twelve, which was a sub-division of Beḷvaḷa Three-Hundred.
The chief Ācharasa seems to have belonged to the Sinda family as in line 35, the
rule of a chief over Kisukāḍu is mentioned. At the end of the inscription is recorded
a gift of flower-garden and oil-mill for worship, etc., to god Chennakēśava by
Dēvaladēvi, the wife of Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Chandarasa, on Friday, the 14th tithi
in the month of Māgha.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(B.K. No. 234 of 1926-27.)
SŪḌI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE STANDING AT THE RIGHT WALL OF THE VILLAGE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
The inscription is for the most part badly damaged and effaced. It belongs
to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva(Vikramāditya VI) and records the grant of
1000 mattar of land in Achaḷapura, made by Mahāsāmanta Daḍiga, son of Guṇḍa
of the Bappura family and Bāli race, who was ruling over Kisukāḍu, to some
one, may be to Kalyāṇaśakti, who was attached to the Nagarēśvara establishment
of the capital town Sūṇḍi for supplying food and giving instruction to ascetics. A
few minor gifts are further recorded of which the first is by a chief (name lost) of
the Bāli race.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 38, Vijaya, which corresponded to
A.D. 1113-14. (Published in Ep. Ind. Vol., XV, pp. 105 ff.)

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(B.K. No. 45 of 1927-28.)
KAKKŪRU, MUṆḌARAGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE MĀRUTI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI), who was ruling the kingdom from his nelevīḍu at Kalyāṇa and introduces his
subordinate Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Permmāḍiyarasa, the governor of Māsavāḍi Hundred
and forty, who sprang from the Yādava family, was the lord of Dvārāvatīpura and
an aṅkakāṟa of Mārtaṇḍadēva. It records the gift of land made by Kāḷigāvuṇḍa
with the sanction of Permmāḍiyarasa to the temple of Mallikārjunadēva built by
Perggaḍe Jōgiśeṭṭi at Kakkūru in Māsavāḍi-nāḍu included in Kuntala-dēśa.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 38, Vijaya, Paushya, paurṇami,
Wednesday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, vyatīpāta = A.D. 1113, December 24, Wednesday.

l.ka: namastuṁgaśiraścuṁbicandracāmaracāravē traiḷōkyanagarāraṁbhamūlastaṁbhāya
śaṁbhavē || namastasmai varāhāya līlayā caratē mahīṁ khurāntaragatō ya-
sya mērōḥ khaṇakhaṇāyatē || svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrīprithvīvallabha
mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cā-

vriddhipravarddhamānamācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ saluttamire kalyāṇada
nelevīḍinū ḷsukhasankathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttumire || svasti samadhigata-

yādavavaṁśōdbhavaṁ | yuvatījanacittōdbhavaṁ | suvarṇnagaruḍadhvajaṁ | vijayakīrti
dhva-

ḷiṁ muṁniṟivaṁ śauryyamaṁ meṟevaṁ jaṁguḷimaṁneyabēṁṭekāṟaṁ mārtaṇḍadēvanaṁ-
kakāṟaṁ | śrēmanmallikārdanadēvapādārādhakaṁ | saka[ḷa*]baḷasādhakaṁ | nāmādisa-
mastapraśastisahitaṁ | śrīmanmahāmaṁḍaḷēśvaraṁ permmāḍiyarasaru mā-
savāḍi nūṟanālvattumaṁ sudharmmadiṁ duṣṭanigrahaṁ śiṣṭapratipāḷanaṁgeydu sukhasaṁ-
kathāvinōdadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyutta mire || vananidhimēkhaḷeyeṁbina manavarataṁ
celvuvetta dhātrīvanitānanado ḷaḷavaṭṭa tiḷakamidenisidu deḷegeseye ne-
gaḻda kuntaḷadēśaṁ || ·enisida kuntaḷadēśado ḷanunayadiṁ māsavāḍināḍoḷe kakkūra
[nava] • • • • • mahimeya nanupama mene negaḻdu nāḍeyuṁ sogayisuguṁ ||

svasti samastaguṇasampannaṁ gōtrapavitraṁ perggaḍe jōgiseṭṭi kakkūra mallikārjjuna
dēvara dēgulamaṁ

māḍisi ·ī dēvargge takka bhūmi vērkku venal kāḷigāvuṁḍa nante geyveneṁdu permmāḍi
yarasargge biṁna-
paṁgeydu cāḷukyavikramava(ri)rṣada 3[8] neya vijayasaṁvatsarada pauṣyada paurṇnamā
sye·u ·uttarāyaṇasaṁkrāṁti vyatīpātadaṁdu dēvara ·aṁgabhōgakkaṁ khaṁḍasphuṭi-
tajīrṇnōddhārakkaṁ [sarvvabā]dhāparihāramāgi biṭṭa bhūmi ·arahaṭada baṭṭeyiṁ
baḍaga ·aragilvāḷa holada mēreya mūḍa haraḷakeyi mattaru 5 ·intī dharmmamaṁ ra-
kṣisidātaṁ paṁnircchāsira kavileyaṁ kōḍuṁkoḷagumaṁ ponnoḷ kaṭṭisi kurukṣētra
prayāgeyoḷ caturvvēdapāragarappa brāhmaṇargge dānaṁgeyda phalamakku || ·ī
dharmmama naḷidātaṁge kurukṣētra vāprayāgeyoḷā caturvvēdapāragarappa
brāhmaṇaruma nerkkōṭi tapōdhanarumaṁ paṁnircchāsira kavileyuma naḷida mahā-
pātakanakkuṁ || svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā yō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ ṣaṣṭirvvarṣa sahaśrā-
ṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ [||*] bahubhi rvvasudhā dattā rājabhissagarādibhiḥ ya-
sya yasya yadā bhūmistasya tasya tadā phaḷaṁ || sāmānyōyaṁ dharmmasēturnripā-
ṇāṁ kālē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ sarvvānētān bhāvinaḥ pārtthivēṁdrānbhū-
yō bhūyō yācatē rāma[caṁdraḥ] [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(B.K. No. 197 of 1926-27.)
JAKKALI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE LEANING AGAINST THE FRONT WALL OF THE TEMPLE OF KALAMĒŚVARA.
inscription.p:
This inscription which is badly damaged refers itself to the reign of a Chāḷukya
king whose name is completely obliterated on the stone, but who must be, from the
date given, no other than Vikramāditya VI. It seems to register a gift of land, the
details of which are lost.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year [40], Hēmaḷambi, • • • • •
• • • • Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, vyatīpāta. In the given cyclic year (= Śaka 1039),
Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa fell on Monday, 24th December A.D. 1117, on which day
the tithi was Pausha amāvāsyā, which commenced at ·03 and ended at ·99 of the day.

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(B.K. No. 61 of 1926-27.)
YALI-SIRŪR, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP NEAR THE VILLAGE CHĀVAḌI.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and records the gift of one arecanut garden made by the eight Gāvuṇḍas and
the Sixty Families of Eleya-Sirivura to the temple of Beṭṭēśvara, when Mahāpradhāna,
Daṇḍanāyaka Nāgavarmayya-Nāyaka was governing Beḷvala Three-Hundred,
Puligeṟe Three-Hundred and Banavāsi Twelve-Thousand provinces at the command
of Mahāpradhāna, Sēnādhipati, Maneverggaḍe, Daṇḍanāyaka Sāyipayya. Nāga-
varmayya-Nāyaka was administering the same provinces in A.D. 1115-16 and
1117-18 (Bomb. Gaz., vol. I, pt. II, p. 452).

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 42, Viḷambi, Jyēshṭha, śu. puṇṇami,
Thursday. In the given year which corresponded to Śaka 1040, the tithi ended at
·51 on Wednesday, 5th June, A.D. 1118, which is probably the English equivalent of
the date. The week-day quoted is apparently wrong citation.

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(B.K. No. 196 of 1926-27.)
JAKKALI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB STANDING IN FRONT OF THE SAṄGAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) who was ruling the kingdom from the nelevīḍu of Jayantīpura
and introduces his subordinate, a Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara who was governing certain
specified tracts including Keḷavāḍi Three-Hundred and the agrahāra Jakkili.
It records the gift of land made to the temple of Sōmēśvara attached to the maṭha
outside the village, by the officer in the presence of the Two-Hundred Mahājanas
of the place.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama [year 43, Śārvari], Kārtika, śu. 1, Sunday,
solar eclipse, which regularly corresponds to Sunday, 24th October, A.D. 1120. The
tithi was actually Āśvina amāvāsyā on which day Kārttika śu. 1 commenced at ·21
and ended the next day at ·17. A solar eclipse occurred on this day.

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(B.K. No. 2 of 1928-29.)
AIHOḶE, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE TEMPLE CALLED CHARANTIGUḌI.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and introduces a certain Kēśavayya-Śeṭṭi, son of Jēmapāryya and Jātiyakka, as a
‘son’ i.e. servant of the Five-Hundred Svāmins of Ayyāvoḷe. It records that Kēśava
constructed a hall (paṭṭaśāle), the eastern and western basadis and a well, consecrated
the Lōkapāḷas and granted land, house-sites and oil-mills for the worship of the
deities.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 44, Vikāri, Vaiśākha, śu. 3, Monday,
corresponding to A.D. 1119, April 14, Monday; ·93.

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(B.K. No. 193 of 1926-27).
KŌḌIKOP, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP INSIDE THE TEMPLE OF MŪLA-BRAHMĒŚVARA.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and mentions his capital Jayantīpura. It records a gift of twelve mattar of land
made for the benefit of the god Mollēśvaradēva of Kiṟu-Nareyaṁgal, attached to the
temple of Śōbhanēśvaradēva of Hiriya-Nareyaṁgal, by Mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara Ācharasa
of the Kusuva family, who was governing the Kisukāḍu Seventy division and several
towns including Nareyaṁgal, the chief of the Twelve (villages), Abbegeṟe and others
in Beḷvola Three-Hundred. The gift was maintained by his eldest son Hemmāḍidēva.
Among the boundaries of the gift land occur the names of Hiriya-Nareyaṁgal
(modern Naregal) and Ummachige (modern Umachgi).

The inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 45, Śubhakṛit, Chaitra, śu. 8,
Monday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti. In Śubhakṛit (= Śaka 1044), Chaitra śu. 8 fell
on Friday, March 17, A.D. 1122, while in the previous year, i.e. Plava (= Śaka 1043),
the cited tithi occurred on Monday = March 28, A.D. 1121. The latter is probably
the intended date. On neither of these days was there a saṅkrānti.

In the text of this record published by Dr. Fleet in J.B.B.R.A.S., Vol XI, pp.
247 f. the following material corrections may be made:

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(B.K. No. 241 of 1928-29.)
NĀVAḶḶI, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF KALAMĒŚVARA.
inscription.p:
This inscription which is very badly damaged belongs to the reign of
Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikrāmaditya VI) and introduces an official (name lost) as
the governor of Beḷvala Three-Hundred and Puligeṟe Three-Hundred districts. It
states that Daṇḍanāyaka Sāyipayya visited the temple of Svayaṁbhū Kalidēva
at the request of Ballarasa and granted in conjunction with the Karaṇas, the
agrahāra village Nārppoḷe as Paramēśvara-datti, for the worship of the god, repairs
to the temple, students' stipends and the maintenance of the Bāla-śikshe.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 46, Plava, Chaitra, puṇṇami,
Monday, lunar eclipse, which regularly correspond to A.D. 1121, April 4, Monday.
On this day there was a lunar eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(B.K. No. 68 of 1926-27.)
SIRŌḶ, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB LYING IN FRONT OF THE ŚIVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription which is damaged and mutilated belongs to the reign of Tribhu-
vanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI) and refers to the rule of Mahāsāmanta Mallarasa
of the Sinda family and Kūnnula race, who is described as the lord of the city of
Bhōgāvatī and the champion of his father (Ayyan-aṅkakāṟa). It registers the gift
of a garden made by three Gāvuṇḍas to the god Mūlasthānadēva, of cess by
the Five Hundred merchants and the Nakara, the Dōṇi One-Thousand and Ugura
Three-Hundred (bodies). It also records similar gifts made to the same god by the
toll officers, Karaṇas, and the betel-leaf merchants (Tambuliga Seṭṭis).

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 46, Plava, [Jyēshṭha, śu.] 8, Thursday,
which regularly corresponds to A.D. 1121, May 26, Thursday; ·68.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(B.K. No. 84 of 1927-28.)
ĀLŪR, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE NĀGĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikra-
māditya VI) and introduces the Daṇḍanāyaka Surigeya Hermmāḍiyarasa, son of
Mādirāja, a ‘moon to the Karṇāṭa family’, as administering the Mahāgrahāra
village Māḷadālūru by the favour of the king. His maternal uncle Mahāpradhāna
Pasāyita, Surigeya Nāgarasa, son of Vāsudēva and grandson of Chāmarasa, granted
a piece of land together with some gold, house-sites, etc., to the Mahājanas
for the maintenance of a satra, a school for the study of Ṛigvēda, a tank and a
water-shed, etc.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 48, Śōbhakṛit, Māgha, puṇṇami,
Friday, lunar eclipse, vyatīpāta. The English equivalent is A.D. 1124, February 1,
which was a day of lunar eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(B.K. No. 86 of 1927-28.)
ĀLŪR, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE VĪRABHADRADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription in Kannada interspersed with a few Sanskrit verses
belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditva VI) and introduces his
officer, Mahāmātya, Antaḥpurādhyaksha, Surigeya Permmāḍiyarasa, son of Surigeya
Mādhava and Muddiyakka, as administering Māḷa[dālūru] by the favour of the king.
It records the gift of land and money made by the latter officer to the Two-Hundred
Mahājanas of the place for the worship of a goddess (name lost) and the mainte-
nance of the Ṛigvēda khaṇḍika (school for teaching Ṛigvēda).

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year [49], [Krōdhi], Śrāvaṇa, puṇṇami,
[Sunday]. The puṇṇami quoted in the inscription commenced on Sunday, 27th July,
A.D. 1124 at ·13 and ended the next day at ·20 of the day. There occurred, however,
a lunar eclipse on the latter day, i.e. Monday.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(B.K. No. 69 of 1927-28.)
DAMBAḶ, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON THE BACK OF THE IMAGE OF THE GOD MĀRUTI IN THE MĀRUTI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya VI)
and introduces his subordinate Permabhūpa as the governor of Māsavāḍi-nāḍd
It states that a certain Ajjaya-Nāyaka, son of Rebbarasa and Bāvikabbe, constructed
a temple of Śiva at Dharmavoḷal to which Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Hermmāḍiyarasa
granted land after purchase from the Sixteen Śeṭṭis and the Nagaras of Dharmavoḷal.
Mention is made of Nirvāṇaśakti-Paṇḍitadēva and of his disciple whose name is lost.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 49, Krōdhi, Māgha, puṇṇami, Monday,
Uttarāyāṇa-saṅkrānti, vyatīpāta, probably corresponding to A.D. 1126, January, 11,
Monday. The Uttarāyaṇa-sankrānti had, however, occurred on 24th December, A.D.
1125. Both the Chāḷukya-Vikrama year and the cyclic year were expired ones.
In Krōdhi (= Śaka 1046), Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti occurred on Wednesday, following
Monday, the pūrṇimā of the month Pausha and not Māgha as cited in the
inscription. The English equivalent of the date would in this case be Wednesday,
December 24, A.D. 1124.

śrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmatribhuvanamalladēvara vijaya-

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(B.K. No. 218 of 1926-27.)
ABBIGĒRI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE SET UP IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF SUṬṬA-BASAPPA.
inscription.p:
This badly damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) and seems to record gift of land made by Mahāmaṇḍa-
ḷēśvara Ācharasa on the representation of the Sixty Okkal to Mallikārjuna Paṇḍita
for the benefit of the temple of Sōmēśvara at Abbaygeṟe in [Narayaṁg]al-Twelve.
Mention is made of Kisukāḍu.

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 50, Viśvā[vasu], Vaiśākha, amāvāsye,
Monday, solar eclipse, vyatīpāta. The English equivalent of the date is probably
Monday, 4th May, A.D. 1125. There was, however, no solar eclipse on this day.

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 458 of 1926.)
MUGAD, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP ON THE TANK BUND.
inscription.p:
This inscription refers itself to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva (Vikramāditya
VI) and is dated Śaka 1047, Viśvāvasu, Pushya, śu. 5, Friday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti.
The date appears to be irregular. Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti in Viśvāvasu fell on Friday,
25th December, A.D. 1125; but the tithi was ba. 14 and not śu. 5 as stated. The
record states that Mahāsāmanta Bammadēva had the grant of land previously made
by his uncle Mahāsāmanta Mārtaṇḍayya for the upkeep of a tank inscribed on stone.
Bammarasa and his cousin Nākarasa were descended from Chāvuṇḍarāya, the
Nārgāvuṇḍa of Muguda Thirty, situated in Mahārājavāḍi-nāḍu, a sub-division of
Palasige Twelve-Thousand. Tribhuvanamalla Gūvaladēva of the Kadaṁba family is
stated to have been governing Palasige Twelve-Thousand and Koṅkaṇa Nine-Hundred
at the time of the grant. The record was composed by the poet Nāgārjuna-Paṇḍita.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(B.K. No. 62 of 1927-28.)
CHIKKAVAḌAVAṬṬI, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE KALAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This damaged inscription belongs to the reign of Tribhuvanamalladēva
(Vikramāditya VI) and states that Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kakkarasa was administering
Suṟuka-Twelve and Avagavāḍi-Twelve, etc. It records the gift of land made by this
officer for the benefit of the temple of Kalidēvasvāmin at Oḍavaḍḍi, to Rūvāri-
Bammōja and to Brahmapuri Nāraṇa-Bhaṭṭa. Oḍavaḍḍi which is evidently the
modern Chikkavaḍavaṭṭi, is stated to be the capital of Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Kakkarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(B.K. No. 39 of 1928-29.)
ARASIBĪḌI, HUNGUND TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A MUTILATED SLAB LYING IN SŪḶĒRAGUḌI TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription is incised on another fragment of the slab containing the inscrip-
tion published by Dr. Barnett in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. XVII, p. 122. It
records the gift of money derived from taxes made by Suṅkaverggaḍe Mantara
Barmmaṇa, an officer under Barmmadēvarasa, son of Sindarasa of the Sinda family,
described as the lord of the city of Bhōgāvatī, for the feeding of the Ṛishis and the
Ajjis (nuns) attached to the Jaina temple called Goṇada Beḍaṅgi Jinālaya of the
capital city Vikramapura (modern Arasibīḍi.)

The record is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 10, Krōdhana, Āshāḍha, śu. 1,
Wednesday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkrānti. The date regularly corresponds to Wednesday,
25th June, A.D. 1085. The lunar month was Nija Āshāḍha.

dhāni vikramapurada śrīgoṇadabeḍangijinālayade riṣiya rajjiya rāhāradā-
nakke ·alliya menneyada suṁkadeluṁ pannāyada suṁkadaluṁ tiṁgaḷige ·aydu pa-
ṇa ḷekkade varṣaṁprati koṭṭa gadyāṇavāṟuma nāsuṁkama nārorvvar nōrppavar ku-
ḍutaṁ barpparī dharmmade paripantanakke nagaramahājana [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(B.K. No. 268 of 1927-28.)
KOP, BĀGALKŌṬ TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB LYING BEHIND THE TEMPLE OF DURGAVVA.
inscription.p:
This mutilated record belongs to the period of Vikramāditya VI and mentions
Perggaḍe Muddarasa, who is described as endowed with the office of the Mahāmātya
(a great minister). It mentions rājadhānī Vikramapura. We know from inscriptions
that Arasibīḍi in the Hungund Taluk of the Bijapur District, was known as
Vikramapura and as the secondary capital of the Western Chālukyas (See Epigraphia
Indica, Vol. XV, p. 82). Since a portion of the inscription is chiselled away, it is not
possible to make out the object of the record.

The inscription bears the date Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 10, Krōdhana, Āshāḍha,
śu. 1, Wednesday, Dakshiṇāyana-saṅkrānti. Its English equivalent is A.D. 1085,
June 25, Wednesday; ·82.

tsarada
·āṣāḍha su 1 budhavāraṁ dakṣiṇāyanasaṁkrāntiyandu rājadhāni vikramapurada śrī-

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(B.K. No. 77 of 1927-28.)
DŌṆI, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE SIDDHALIṄGADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
The inscription registers the gift of certain annual taxes made by Pannāyada-suṅka-
vergaḍe Viṭṭarasa, at the command of Sarvādhikāri, Manevergaḍe, Mahāprachaṇḍa-
Daṇḍanāyaka, Mahādēva-Bhaṭṭa to Śaktiśiva-Paṇḍita, for the benefit of the temple
of Siddhēśvaradēva, in the presence of the Sixty Mahājanas headed by the Ūroḍeya
of Drōṇāpura and of the several Seṭṭis. A gift of money made by the toll officer
Mādirāja and Mulihi-Seṭṭi is also recorded. Further, after praising the Body of
Ugura Three-Hundred, it records gifts of certain duties made by them to the same
Śaktiśiva-Paṇḍita for the benefit of the temple. Śaktiśiva was the disciple of
Lakshmīdhara-Paṇḍita and the āchārya of the temple.

The inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 19, Bhāva, Pushya, śu. 5,
Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti, vyatīpāta. The saṅkrānti occurred not on śu. 5
(which was Friday), but on śu. 15 which was a Sunday = A.D. 1094, December 24,
on which day it commenced at ·67.

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(B.K. No. 78 of 1927-28.)
DŌṆI, MUṆḌARGI PĒṬHĀ, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB BUILT INTO THE WALL OF THE SIDDHALIṄGADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 23, Bahudhānya, Pushya,
amāvāsye, Sunday, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkramaṇa, Vyatīpāta, corresponding probably to
A.D. 1098, December 25, Saturday, which was the day of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti; the
week day cited in the record seems to be an error. The epigraph registers a grant of
tax-free land made by the Mahājanas, who were headed by the Ūroḍeya, and the Seṭṭis
of Drōṇāpura to the god Siddhēśvaradēva. Śaktiśiva-Paṇḍita, the āchārya of the
temple, received the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(B.K. No. 243 of 1927-28.)
SHIRŪR, BĀGALKŌṬ TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE SIDDHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 24, Pramāthin, Phālguṇa, ba. 5,
Sunday. The date appears to be irregular. In Pramāthin, Phālguṇa ba. 5 fell on
Friday = A.D. 1100, March 2. If ba. 5 is a mistake for ba. 7, the equivalent would
be A.D. 1100, March 4, Sunday. The inscription registers a grant of land made by
Prasannarāśi-Paṇḍita, the āchārya of the temple of Siddhēśvaradēva, to the tank
called Bāchiseṭṭiyakeṟe. The plot of land granted formed part of the property of
the god.

paṇṇitadēvaru dēvara poladoḷage bāciseṭṭiya keṟege biṭṭa keṟeya keḷage gē-
ṇa kariya keyi mattar 4 [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(B.K. No. 79 of 1928-29.)
GALGALI, BĪḶGI PĒṬHĀ, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A BROKEN STONE BUILT INTO THE WALL OF A HOUSE NEAR THE
PARVATADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription is dated Bīra-Vikrama year 26, Vishu,
• • • • śu 1, Sunday, probably corresponding to A.D. 1101, July 18,
Sunday, the month being Adhika-Bhādrapada (not verifiable). It seems to record the
death of Kannaseṭṭi in a raid. The district of Tarddavāḍi and the village Niḍuvaṇi
are mentioned. Bīra-Vikrama year apparently stands for Chālukya-Vikrama year.

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(B.K. No. 221 of 1928-29.)
BYĀLHĀḶ, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE KALAMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This much damaged inscription is dated [Chāḷukya] Vikrama year 27, Chitra-
[bhānu], the other details being lost.In the list of B.K. inscriptions appended to the Annual Report on South Indian Epigraphy,
1928-29, this inscription has been wrongly assigned to the reign of Jagadēkamalladēva.The date corresponds to A.D. 1102-03.

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(B.K. No. 238 of 1928-29.)
MORAB, NAVALGUND TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE HANUMANTADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription, whose beginning is worn out, is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year
28, Subhānu, Dīpāvaḷi-amavāse, Sunday, Vyatīpāta. In Subhānu, Dīpāvaḷi-amāvāsyā
i.e., Āśvina-amāvāsyā ended on Saturday = A.D. 1103, October 3. Probably the
gift was recorded on the next day. A grant of land appears to have been made.
Mallikārjuna-Paṇḍita is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(B.K. No. 32 of 1926-27.)
LAKKUṆḌI, GADAG TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA OF THE SŌMĒŚVARA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 28, Subhānu, Māgha, ba. 8,
Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1104, January 21, Thursday; f.d.t. ·71. It registers
a gift of cloth made by Saṅkimayya-Nāyaka and Mahādēva-Bhaṭṭa.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(B.K. No. 166 of 1928-29.)
NĪLAGUND, BADAMI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.
ON A HERO SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE RĀMALIṄGADĒVA TEMPLE.
inscription.p:
This inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 3[1, Vya]ya, [Śrāva]ṇa, śu. 1[0],
Thursday, corresponding to A.D. 1106, July 12, Thursday. It records the death of
Chāva-Gāvuṇḍa and Holli-Gāvuṇḍa in a battle on the occasion of a campaign on
Nīlagunde by Chāgaladēvi, wife of a Mahāsāmanta (name lost) of Toṟagale.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(B.K. No. 204 of 1926-27.)
NIḌGUNDI, ROṆ TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.
ON A STONE INSIDE THE TEMPLE OF BASAVAṆṆA.
inscription.p:
This inscription is dated Chāḷukya-Vikrama year 31, Vyaya, Śrāvaṇa, puṇṇami,
Monday, lunar eclipse, corresponding to A.D. 1106, July 16, Monday, f.d.t. ·92; the
eclipse, however, occurred on the following Tuesday. The record states that gifts
of gold were made by several persons and the Four Hundred Mahājanas of Niḍugundi
for the pavitrārōhaṇa of the god Kēśavādityadēva and for the pārāyaṇa of two
Vēdas.
